scenarioName: Happy path: Full Booking Allocation with CAPTAIN+VICE duo assignment
objective: Validate the complete end-to-end booking allocation flow with proper CAPTAIN/VICE_CAPTAIN duo assignment, from user signup through booking creation and verification of companion assignments and roster slot updates.
preconditions: Mailpit email server running on localhost:8025
Test venue exists or can be created

venue.strategy: Use existing test venue or first available venue from GET /venues
venue.fallback: Create test venue if needed via internal API

booking.note: Ensure roster slots exist for this date/time

steps.layman: Register a new client user account to simulate a customer wanting to make a booking.
Wait for the verification email to arrive at Mailpit so we can extract the email verification token.
validations: Email body contains verification token or link

dataExtraction.clientVerifyToken: Extract token from email body or verification link

steps.layman: Verify the client's email address using the token from the email to activate the account.
Log in as the client to get an authentication token for making authenticated requests.
Register the first companion user who will be assigned to the booking (either as CAPTAIN or VICE_CAPTAIN).
Wait for Companion A's verification email to extract the verification token.
validations: Email recipient matches companion A email
Email body contains verification token or link

dataExtraction.companionAVerifyToken: Extract token from email body or verification link

steps.layman: Verify Companion A's email address to activate their companion account.
Log in as Companion A to get their authentication token.
Retrieve Companion A's profile to verify their designation (CAPTAIN or VICE_CAPTAIN) was assigned during signup.
validations: Response designation is either CAPTAIN or VICE_CAPTAIN

steps.layman: Register the second companion user who will be assigned to the booking with the opposite designation from Companion A.
Wait for Companion B's verification email to extract the verification token.
validations: Email recipient matches companion B email
Email body contains verification token or link

dataExtraction.companionBVerifyToken: Extract token from email body or verification link

steps.layman: Verify Companion B's email address to activate their companion account.
Log in as Companion B to get their authentication token.
Retrieve Companion B's profile to verify their designation was assigned and is opposite to Companion A.
validations: Response designation is either CAPTAIN or VICE_CAPTAIN
Response designation is different from Companion A's designation

steps.layman: Search for available venues to find a suitable location for the booking.
validations: At least one venue is returned

dataExtraction.testVenueId: Select first venue or venue matching test criteria

steps.layman: Populate roster slots for Companion A at the test venue to ensure they have availability for the booking date.
validations: Number of slots created is at least 4

steps.layman: Populate roster slots for Companion B at the test venue to ensure they also have availability for the booking date.
validations: Number of slots created is at least 4

steps.layman: Query the database to verify roster slots were created with AVAILABLE status for both companions.
steps.query: SELECT id, companionProfileId, venueId, startTime, endTime, status FROM roster_slots WHERE venueId = {{TEST_VENUE_ID}} AND date = '2026-05-10' AND status = 'AVAILABLE'
validations: At least 8 roster slots exist (4 per companion minimum)
Slots exist for both companion profile IDs

steps.layman: Check available booking start times for the test venue on the booking date to confirm slots are visible to the client.
validations: Response contains array of available start times
Available start times include 14:00 or nearby times
At least 2 start times are available

dataExtraction.selectedStartTime: 14:00 or first available start time from response

steps.layman: Create a booking for the client at the test venue to trigger the allocation engine to assign companions.
Query the database to verify exactly 2 companion assignments were created for the booking.
steps.query: SELECT id, bookingId, companionProfileId, designation, presenceStatus, selfMatchStatus, clientMatchStatus FROM booking_companion_assignments WHERE bookingId = {{BOOKING_ID}}
validations: Both assignments reference the same bookingId

steps.layman: Verify that one assignment has designation CAPTAIN and the other has VICE_CAPTAIN to confirm proper duo allocation.
steps.query: SELECT designation, COUNT(*) as count FROM booking_companion_assignments WHERE bookingId = {{BOOKING_ID}} GROUP BY designation
validations: One assignment has designation CAPTAIN with count 1
One assignment has designation VICE_CAPTAIN with count 1
No other designations exist for this booking

steps.layman: Verify that the two assigned companions are different users, not the same companion assigned twice.
steps.query: SELECT DISTINCT companionProfileId FROM booking_companion_assignments WHERE bookingId = {{BOOKING_ID}}
validations: Both companionProfileIds match either Companion A or Companion B profile IDs
No companion is assigned more than once to the same booking

steps.layman: Join assignment records with companion profiles to verify the designation in the assignment matches the companion's profile designation.
steps.query: SELECT bca.designation as assignmentDesignation, cp.designation as profileDesignation, bca.companionProfileId FROM booking_companion_assignments bca JOIN companion_profiles cp ON bca.companionProfileId = cp.id WHERE bca.bookingId = {{BOOKING_ID}}
validations: Each assignment designation matches the companion profile designation
CAPTAIN assignment references companion profile with designation CAPTAIN
VICE_CAPTAIN assignment references companion profile with designation VICE_CAPTAIN

steps.layman: Verify that roster slots for both assigned companions were updated to BOOKED status with the booking ID reference.
steps.query: SELECT id, companionProfileId, status, bookingId, startTime, endTime FROM roster_slots WHERE companionProfileId IN ({{COMPANION_A_PROFILE_ID}}, {{COMPANION_B_PROFILE_ID}}) AND venueId = {{TEST_VENUE_ID}} AND date = '2026-05-10' AND startTime >= '14:00:00' AND endTime <= '16:00:00'
validations: At least 4 slots per companion have status BOOKED (to cover 2-hour booking)
All BOOKED slots reference the correct bookingId
Both companions have slots booked for the same time range
No slots remain AVAILABLE for the booked time range for these companions

steps.layman: Retrieve the full booking details via the API to verify all booking information is accessible to the client.
validations: Response booking id matches created booking id
Response endTime is 2 hours after startTime

steps.layman: Perform a final database check to confirm the booking record is in the expected state with all required fields populated.
steps.query: SELECT id, clientUserId, venueId, status, startTime, endTime, qrCode, pinCode, bookingColor, companionMatchQrCode, companionMatchPinCode FROM bookings WHERE id = {{BOOKING_ID}}
validations: clientUserId matches the client user id
venueId matches the test venue id

postConditions: Client user created, verified, and logged in
Two companion users created, verified, logged in, with opposite designations
Roster slots populated for both companions at test venue
One assignment is CAPTAIN, one is VICE_CAPTAIN
Assignment designations match companion profile designations
Roster slots updated to BOOKED with correct bookingId

api: All login requests return 200 with accessToken
Venue search returns at least one venue
Availability check returns available start times
Booking creation returns 201 with CONFIRMED status
db: Exactly 2 BookingCompanionAssignment records exist for booking
Assignment designations match companion profile designations
Roster slots updated to BOOKED status
Booking record has all required fields populated
mustNotOccur: More than 2 companion assignments for the booking
Same companion assigned twice to the same booking
Assignment designation mismatch with companion profile designation
Roster slots remain AVAILABLE after booking creation

manual: Delete users for client and companions
cleanupStrategy.note: Cleanup is optional for isolated test runs. Database may be reset between test executions.

notes: This test validates the core allocation engine requirement: proper CAPTAIN + VICE_CAPTAIN duo assignment
The test uses Mailpit for email verification, requiring it to be running and accessible
Internal API endpoints require INTERNAL_API_TOKEN authentication
Roster population must occur before availability check and booking creation
The test assumes companions are assigned designations during signup/profile creation
Booking duration is assumed to be 2 hours (default per SDS)
Time zone is Asia/Dubai (+04:00) for all timestamp handling
RUN_ID placeholder must be replaced with unique identifier during test execution
Test can be run multiple times with different RUN_ID values without conflicts
